sections.paragraph:
πανδοχεύς, έως, ὁ (πᾶς, δέχομαι; Polyb. 2, 15, 6 and Plut., Mor. 130e in mss.; Iambl., Vi. Pyth. 33, 238 [LDeubner, SBBerlAk ’39 XIX p. 15]. The Att. πανδοκεύς in Pla., Leg. 11, 918b et al.; Epict. 1, 24, 14; Polyb. [Büttner-W.] and Plut. [Paton-Wegehaupt 1925], loc. cit. in the text. Taken over by Jews [Billerb. II 183f], but not found in Philo and Joseph.—B-D-F §33; W-S. §5, 27e; Mlt-H. 108) inn-keeper Lk 10:35.—Kl. Pauly V 1384–86; BHHW II 693f. M-M.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Att. Att. = Attic, esp. in ref. to dialect

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 754
